summary:
The presence of a sinus in a nonvital pulp suggests ongoing infection due to odontogenic origin. Surgical curettage of the sinus is required to definitively clear infective tissue and restore normal anatomy, making it the most appropriate initial step in managing this condition.

Note: Do not include any commentary about correctness; only summarize what was reasoned by Agent1.
